skill.name: stride-analysis-patterns
skill.description: Apply the STRIDE methodology to identify threats, map them to assets and trust boundaries, and document mitigations.
skill.summary: Create structured STRIDE threat models for architecture and security reviews.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, STRIDE terminology, sample Python snippets, and documentation links. Manual review found no executable hooks, command execution, malicious network behavior, prompt injection, weak cryptography recommendation, or data exfiltration pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Marked as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static Ruby backtick detections are Markdown code fences and educational examples inside SKILL.md. They do not execute commands, invoke a shell, or process user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown delimiters for diagrams or Python examples. There is no runtime file, shell invocation, or command string outside documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Detector Matched STRIDE Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography detections are from words such as description, destination, design, and encryption-related STRIDE examples. The skill recommends TLS, secure tokens, HMAC, signatures, and encryption instead of weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual context shows ordinary prose fields and safe threat-modeling examples, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic implementation. No weak algorithm is recommended or executed.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ransomware Extension Detector Matched a Field Name
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The ransomware file-extension hit is the sample Python attribute flow.encrypted in a function that identifies unencrypted trust-boundary crossings. It is not a file extension, file write, or encryption routine.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a boolean attribute check in documentation. There is no filesystem access, payload, encryption loop, or ransom behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Marked as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs are resource links to Microsoft, OWASP, and Wiley documentation. The skill does not fetch these links, transmit data, or instruct automated network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a Resources section. No code path, client, webhook, or secret-bearing request is present.

content.value_statement: Security teams need a repeatable way to identify threats before designs ship. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through STRIDE categories, risk scoring, and mitigation documentation.
actual_capabilities: Explains each STRIDE category and its related control family.
Provides a structured threat model document template.
Supplies sample Python classes for organizing threats, assets, and trust boundaries.
Maps data-flow elements and interactions to applicable STRIDE categories.
Suggests common mitigations for spoofing, tampering, repudiation, disclosure, denial of service, and privilege risks.
limitations: Does not inspect running systems or verify deployed controls.
Requires accurate architecture, assets, and trust-boundary details from the user.
Does not replace penetration testing or formal security review.
Example code is educational and may need adaptation before production use.

use_cases.title: Review a New Service Design
use_cases.description: Identify likely threats, affected assets, and mitigations before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Prepare Audit Documentation
use_cases.description: Create a clear threat model with risk scores, recommendations, and evidence for review.

use_cases.title: Run a Team Threat Modeling Session
use_cases.description: Guide developers through STRIDE questions for each component and interaction.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic STRIDE Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the STRIDE analysis patterns skill to review this system: [describe system]. Identify assets, trust boundaries, and one threat per STRIDE category.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Data Flow Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Threat modeling from components and flows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply STRIDE to these components and data flows: [paste diagram or list]. Highlight trust-boundary crossings and likely threats.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Mitigations
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert threats into an action plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the STRIDE risk matrix to rank these threats: [paste threat list]. Recommend immediate, short-term, and long-term mitigations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Complete Threat Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation for review or audit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete STRIDE threat model for this architecture: [paste architecture]. Include assets, assumptions, threats, likelihood, impact, mitigations, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Review a web application with users, an API gateway, backend services, and a database.
output_examples.output: A STRIDE threat model with assets, trust boundaries, spoofing risks, tampering risks, disclosure risks, denial-of-service risks, privilege risks, and mitigation priorities.

output_examples.input: Analyze a payment workflow that crosses from the public internet to internal services.
output_examples.output: A prioritized list of trust-boundary threats, likely attack paths, audit logging needs, encryption requirements, and follow-up questions for the design team.

output_examples.input: Prepare a security review summary for a new API.
output_examples.output: A concise review covering critical assets, top threats, risk scores, immediate controls, owner assignments, and documentation gaps.

best_practices: Provide system diagrams, assets, data sensitivity, and trust boundaries before analysis.
Review every STRIDE category even when one category seems less relevant.
Turn each high-priority threat into a mitigation owner and target date.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the generated threat list as complete without stakeholder review.
Do not skip low-likelihood threats when the impact is critical.
Do not use example code as production security tooling without validation.

faq.question: What is STRIDE?
faq.answer: STRIDE is a threat modeling method covering spoofing, tampering, repudiation, information disclosure, denial of service, and elevation of privilege.

faq.question: Does this skill scan code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured analysis patterns, templates, and example logic for threat modeling.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide architecture details, data flows, assets, trust boundaries, user roles, and known assumptions.

faq.question: Can it help with compliance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize threats, mitigations, and risk decisions into review-ready documentation.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for penetration testing?
faq.answer: No. It helps identify design threats, but testing is still needed to validate real implementations.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
